name: Tres Cerros Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – circular desde La Fabriquilla por los Cerros San Miguel, Testa y los Carlos
description: Hike the moderate 5.5-mile Tres Cerros Trail in Cabo de Gata-Níjar, gaining 1623 feet for panoramic views and coastal scenery.
region_intro: Hiking around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar offers diverse terrain shaped by its volcanic origin and coastal environment. The region features dramatic volcanic rock formations, vertiginous cliffs, and unspoiled white-sand beaches along its coastline. Inland, hikers encounter semi-desert landscapes with arid plains and unique dwarf vegetation. This UNESCO Global Geopark provides a varied backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – Castle of San Pedro from Las Negras, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This coastal path leads to the historic Castle of San Pedro. Another top favourite among local hikers is Morrón de los Genoveses Loop Trail, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route offers views of the coastline and access to secluded beaches. Local hikers also love the Senda Azul de Cabo de Gata Trail, a 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail leading through the park's volcanic interior, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar is defined by volcanic formations, pristine coastlines, and semi-desert interiors. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1700 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: There are over 230 hiking routes available in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 96 easy, 109 moderate, and 28 difficult trails, ensuring a diverse range of options for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: The terrain in Cabo de Gata-Níjar is incredibly diverse, shaped by its volcanic origin and coastal environment. You'll encounter dramatic volcanic rock formations, vertiginous cliffs, and unspoiled white-sand beaches. Inland, the landscape transitions to semi-desert areas with arid plains and unique dwarf vegetation. It's a UNESCO Global Geopark, offering fascinating geological features like lava flows, calderas, and fossilized reef structures.

Q: What is the best time to hike in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: The best time to hike in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the occasional cooler, wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the park's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the park is rich in historical sites and landmarks. You can hike to the historic &lt;strong&gt;Castle of San Pedro&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the ethnographic &lt;strong&gt;Molino de los Genoveses&lt;/strong&gt; near Playa de los Genoveses, or discover the old gold mines of Rodalquilar. Other notable points include ancient watchtowers like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Tower, Cabo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;, and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/736304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Gata Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: What natural features can I discover while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover a wealth of natural features, including unique volcanic formations, dramatic cliffs, and pristine beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Genoveses Beach&lt;/a&gt;. The park is also home to the ecologically vital coastal salt flats (Las Salinas de Cabo de Gata), where you can observe numerous bird species, including flamingos. Geological wonders like the &lt;strong&gt;Petrified Wave&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Monsul Dune&lt;/strong&gt; at Playa de Monsul are also popular attractions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: The trails in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the unique volcanic landscapes, and the opportunity to discover secluded coves and beaches. The diverse environments, from arid plains to dramatic cliffs, are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: Yes, National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar offers several easy trails suitable for families. These routes often lead to accessible beaches or offer gentle walks through picturesque landscapes, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty without strenuous effort. Many trails are well-maintained and provide opportunities for children to explore safely.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While many natural parks in Spain have restrictions, dogs are generally permitted on trails in Cabo de Gata-Níjar, provided they are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. However, specific regulations may apply to certain protected areas or beaches, especially during peak seasons. It's always advisable to check local signage or park guidelines for the most current information.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are numerous circular hiking routes in the park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984513483/morron-de-los-genoveses-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de los Genoveses Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option offering coastal views. Another excellent circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984570228/natural-park-of-cabo-de-gata-circular-from-rodalquilar-via-cerro-del-cinto-and-the-gold-mines" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular from Rodalquilar via Cerro del Cinto and the Gold Mines&lt;/a&gt;, which combines natural beauty with historical exploration.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in the park?
A: Parking is generally available at or near the trailheads of popular routes and in the charming villages within the park. However, during peak season, especially in summer, access to some popular beaches and their nearby parking areas may be restricted or require payment. It's recommended to arrive early or consider public transport options when available.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options to reach hiking trailheads within National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar are limited but exist, particularly connecting the main towns and some popular coastal areas. During the summer months, shuttle services often operate to reduce traffic in sensitive areas like the access to Playa de los Genoveses and Playa de Mónsul. It's best to check local bus schedules for specific routes and times.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails in the park?
A: Yes, the long-distance GR92 trail, also known as the Mediterranean Coastal Path, extends through National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar. This trail offers an extensive hiking experience, allowing you to explore significant stretches of the park's stunning coastline and connect various points of interest over multiple days.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the old gold mines?
A: Yes, the park offers trails that delve into its mining history. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984570228/natural-park-of-cabo-de-gata-circular-from-rodalquilar-via-cerro-del-cinto-and-the-gold-mines" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route from Rodalquilar via Cerro del Cinto and the Gold Mines&lt;/a&gt;. This trail allows you to explore the ruins and infrastructure of the old gold mines near Rodalquilar, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and historical insight.
tips: The cisterns were cisterns used by the Arabs to store drinking water. This, predictably, was designed for the watchtower on Cerro Los Carlos.
At the top of this hill you will find the ruins of an old watchtower.

faqs.question: Where is the Tres Cerros Trail located?
faqs.answer: This specific Tres Cerros Trail is located in the beautiful Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata-Níjar, starting from La Fabriquilla Beach. It's a circular route offering stunning coastal views.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near La Fabriquilla Beach. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the beach, but it can get busy during peak season, so arriving early is advisable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of a coastal hike in a natural park. You can expect a mix of paths, some rocky sections, and ascents to viewpoints. Given its moderate difficulty, it involves some elevation gain and descent, but it's generally manageable for regular hikers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Old Aljibe on Sendero La Cruceta and enjoy panoramic views from the Cerro Los Carlos Viewpoint. The starting and ending point, La Fabriquilla Beach, is also a highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Tres Cerros Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this trail is suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. While not overly strenuous, it does involve nearly 500 meters of elevation gain over its 8.8 km length, so it's not ideal for absolute beginners without any hiking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking in Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata-Níjar. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the occasional cooler, windier days of winter. Early mornings are recommended to avoid the midday sun, especially outside of winter.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails within the park, including the Transandalus, Tartera al Collado, Camino Rambla del Corralete, Sendero de los Tres Cerros, Sendero La Cruceta, Sendero a Las Coronillas, and Sendero Rambla del Corralete.

faqs.question: Is the Tres Cerros Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within a Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
faqs.answer: No, there is no entrance fee or specific permit required to hike this trail or access the Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata-Níjar. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing the natural environment.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the coastal environment and moderate difficulty, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), sturdy hiking shoes, and snacks. A camera is also a good idea to capture the scenic views.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, starting from La Fabriquilla Beach, you'll find some basic amenities, especially during the warmer months. These might include small chiringuitos (beach bars) or cafes where you can get refreshments before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Old Aljibe on Sendero La Cruceta
